element_introduction_order.mastery_context: V-22 Osprey crash landing into Black Mesa facility
Standard loadout for initial combat scenarios
Three Marine types: soldier (fire support), medic (healing), engineer (obstacle removal)
Escalating threat separate from original Half-Life aliens
Government black ops sent to destroy base and silence witnesses

element_introduction_order.element: Advanced weaponry (sniper rifle, combat knife, alien weapons)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Specialized tools for varied combat scenarios
Engineer Marines unlock passages; player solves environmental challenges
Continuous world exploration with chapter-based structure

typical_relief_triggers: Allied Marine squad assists in combat
Discovery of weapons or healing items
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating threat curve with alternating combat encounters and environmental navigation; continuous narrative maintains pressure while squad mechanics provide periodic relief

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction through crash landing and initial alien encounters with basic weaponry; player learns movement and combat fundamentals
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of Race X faction and squad mechanics; complexity increases with multiple enemy types and tactical squad coordination required
difficulty_curve.late_game: Black Operations units introduce human opponent variety; combined alien and human threat escalation; higher resource pressure
notable_spikes: Osprey crash sequence establishes immediate danger
First Race X encounter shifts threat profile
Black Operations arrival recontextualizes mission from containment to survival

notable_level_patterns.description: Campaign opens with squad transport shot down; player awakens in facility medical bay, establishing disorientation and isolation as core narrative tension
Player alternates between solo navigation and squad-assisted combat; three Marine archetypes (soldier, medic, engineer) enable different tactical approaches to obstacles
Campaign introduces original Half-Life aliens, then Race X faction, then human Black Operations; creates three-layer enemy complexity
Progress requires solving environmental obstacles (engineer door-cutting) combined with combat encounters; puzzles are gated by resource availability
